## Findings

### F-analyst-ROCBA-DISK-001 — fsstat on rocba-cdrive.e01 reports: File System Type: NTFS; Vol… *(confidence: HIGH)*

fsstat on rocba-cdrive.e01 reports: File System Type: NTFS; Volume Name: Windows; Version: Windows XP; Volume Serial Number: F0E0FE66E0FE3288; Cluster Size: 4096; MFT entry range 0 - 479488.. The evidence is a single NTFS volume from a Windows XP system (no partition table; mmls returned nothing), so user data lives under 'Documents and Settings'. This establishes the forensic baseline for the ROCBA disk triage..

- **Evidence pointer:** `/mnt/evidence/Standard Forensic Case/rocba-cdrive.e01#volume fsstat / VSN F0E0FE66E0FE3288` — tool `fsstat`, command `fsstat "/mnt/evidence/Standard Forensic Case/rocba-cdrive.e01"`
- **Cited evidence:** `NTFS`, `Windows XP`, `F0E0FE66E0FE3288` — every cited token resolves in the real tool output (Citation seat).
- **Integrity:** `output_sha256 5b963eba0121a848…` — re-hash verified; **independently reproducible** with `csift trace --rerun F-analyst-ROCBA-DISK-001`.
- **Council Receipt:** `46207f531b7d2c8d…` — seats: citation=SUPPORTED, tool-semantics=SUPPORTED, contradiction=SUPPORTED, inference=SUPPORTED

### F-analyst-ROCBA-DISK-002 — fls of the fredr user profile (inode 154910) lists, among stand… *(confidence: HIGH)*

fls of the fredr user profile (inode 154910) lists, among standard profile folders, multiple distinct cloud-storage sync directories: 'Google Drive', 'iCloudDrive', 'iCloudPhotos', 'OneDrive', 'OneDrive - Stark Research Labs', 'ROCBA Dropbox', 'Your team Dropbox', plus a 'Stark Research Labs' folder. The profile also contains an NTUSER.DAT registry hive.. The fredr account is provisioned with an unusually broad set of personal and third-party cloud-sync clients (Google Drive, iCloud, two Dropbox roots) alongside the corporate 'OneDrive - Stark Research Labs'. The co-location of corporate and personal cloud-sync roots in one profile gives multiple candidate channels for moving company files off the host; their contents warrant examination. This does not by itself show any file left the host..

- **Evidence pointer:** `/mnt/evidence/Standard Forensic Case/rocba-cdrive.e01#inode 154910 (Users/fredr)` — tool `fls`, command `fls "/mnt/evidence/Standard Forensic Case/rocba-cdrive.e01" 154910`
- **Cited evidence:** `Google Drive`, `iCloudDrive`, `OneDrive - Stark Research Labs`, `ROCBA Dropbox`, `Your team Dropbox`, `Stark Research Labs`, `NTUSER.DAT` — every cited token resolves in the real tool output (Citation seat).
- **Integrity:** `output_sha256 d8d3e2e1a7521258…` — re-hash verified; **independently reproducible** with `csift trace --rerun F-analyst-ROCBA-DISK-002`.
- **Council Receipt:** `15174bfb5ab7918f…` — seats: citation=SUPPORTED, tool-semantics=SUPPORTED, contradiction=SUPPORTED, inference=SUPPORTED

### F-analyst-ROCBA-DISK-003 — fls of the volume root lists modern-Windows directories: 'Users… *(confidence: HIGH)*

fls of the volume root lists modern-Windows directories: 'Users', 'OneDriveTemp', 'Windows10Upgrade', 'Windows.old', and '$Recycle.Bin'. None of these are present on a Windows XP installation.. The installed operating system on this volume is Windows 10 (the 'Users', 'OneDriveTemp', 'Windows10Upgrade' and 'Windows.old' layout is a Windows 10 profile/upgrade structure, not Windows XP). The 'Version: Windows XP' string reported by fsstat in F-analyst-ROCBA-DISK-001 is the NTFS on-disk format version (NTFS 3.1, first shipped with XP) and not the OS edition; that earlier finding's OS wording should be read with this correction..

- **Evidence pointer:** `/mnt/evidence/Standard Forensic Case/rocba-cdrive.e01#volume root directory listing` — tool `fls`, command `fls "/mnt/evidence/Standard Forensic Case/rocba-cdrive.e01"`
- **Cited evidence:** `Users`, `OneDriveTemp`, `Windows10Upgrade`, `Windows.old` — every cited token resolves in the real tool output (Citation seat).
- **Integrity:** `output_sha256 48bfc135a06a7877…` — re-hash verified; **independently reproducible** with `csift trace --rerun F-analyst-ROCBA-DISK-003`.
- **Council Receipt:** `9653b18d5168e0d9…` — seats: citation=SUPPORTED, tool-semantics=SUPPORTED, contradiction=SUPPORTED, inference=SUPPORTED

### F-analyst-ROCBA-DISK-004 — fls of 'Users/fredr/Stark Research Labs/Maria Hill - WorkingFil… *(confidence: HIGH)*

fls of 'Users/fredr/Stark Research Labs/Maria Hill - WorkingFiles' (inode 159702) lists research documents including RareEarthDeposits_Confidential.jpg, German-KITT-Specs.docx, German-KITT-Specs-CMDRHill-Laptop.docx, Chord_Spacetime.pdf, Heisenberg_Uncertainty_Principle_and_the.pdf, and SO_5_non_Fermi_liquid_in_a_Coulomb_box_d.pdf. The directory carries a '.SyncRootIdentity' alternate data stream.. fredr's profile contains a company-context folder ('Stark Research Labs/Maria Hill - WorkingFiles') holding technical research files, one of which is explicitly named RareEarthDeposits_Confidential.jpg and another German-KITT-Specs.docx. The '.SyncRootIdentity' ADS marks the folder as a cloud-synced (OneDrive) location. This places sensitive, named-as-confidential company material in the user's profile; it does not by itself show the material was sent off the host..

- **Evidence pointer:** `/mnt/evidence/Standard Forensic Case/rocba-cdrive.e01#inode 159702 (Users/fredr/Stark Research Labs/Maria Hill - WorkingFiles)` — tool `fls`, command `fls "/mnt/evidence/Standard Forensic Case/rocba-cdrive.e01" 159702`
- **Cited evidence:** `RareEarthDeposits_Confidential.jpg`, `German-KITT-Specs.docx`, `Chord_Spacetime.pdf`, `.SyncRootIdentity` — every cited token resolves in the real tool output (Citation seat).
- **Integrity:** `output_sha256 813a9e4577f499c6…` — re-hash verified; **independently reproducible** with `csift trace --rerun F-analyst-ROCBA-DISK-004`.
- **Council Receipt:** `e1ab563016221d93…` — seats: citation=SUPPORTED, tool-semantics=SUPPORTED, contradiction=SUPPORTED, inference=SUPPORTED

### F-analyst-ROCBA-DISK-005 — icat of inode 1975 (Users/fredr/AppData/Roaming/Microsoft/Windo… *(confidence: HIGH)*

icat of inode 1975 (Users/fredr/AppData/Roaming/Microsoft/Windows/Recent/RareEarthDeposits_Confidential.lnk) piped through strings shows the embedded target path 'C:\Users\fredr\Stark Research Labs\Maria Hill - WorkingFiles\RareEarthDeposits_Confidential.jpg' and the machine name 'srl-forge'.. A Windows Recent shortcut (.lnk) exists for RareEarthDeposits_Confidential.jpg, with an embedded target path resolving to the Stark Research Labs/Maria Hill - WorkingFiles folder. Windows writes a Recent .lnk when a file is opened through the shell, so this indicates the confidential file was opened on the host (machine name srl-forge in the link). The link records access, not copying or transfer..

- **Evidence pointer:** `/mnt/evidence/Standard Forensic Case/rocba-cdrive.e01#inode 1975 (Recent/RareEarthDeposits_Confidential.lnk)` — tool `icat`, command `icat "/mnt/evidence/Standard Forensic Case/rocba-cdrive.e01" 1975 | strings -a -n 5`
- **Cited evidence:** `RareEarthDeposits_Confidential.jpg`, `Maria Hill - WorkingFiles`, `srl-forge` — every cited token resolves in the real tool output (Citation seat).
- **Integrity:** `output_sha256 7c6be0ed1f70c1f0…` — re-hash verified; **independently reproducible** with `csift trace --rerun F-analyst-ROCBA-DISK-005`.
- **Council Receipt:** `c98251a62b9943d4…` — seats: citation=SUPPORTED, tool-semantics=SUPPORTED, contradiction=SUPPORTED, inference=SUPPORTED

## Confidence & limitations

- Confidence is stated per finding (HIGH / MEDIUM / LOW / SPECULATIVE) and reflects what a single artifact
  can bear; nothing here is asserted beyond the cited evidence.
- "Observation" is the literal tool output; "interpretation" is the analyst's inference. The Council's
  Citation, Tool-semantics, Contradiction, and Inference seats independently checked the leap between them.
- Every finding traces to its tool execution (`csift trace F-analyst-ROCBA-DISK-001`) and can be
  re-executed against the evidence (`csift trace --rerun …`); the Council Receipts are hash-chained.
- This narrative is generated from the audit substrate, not hand-written — it cannot contain a claim that
  is not a verified node in the graph.
